title: Did Seele have agents inside NERV
display_title: Did Seele have agents inside NERV
wikitext:
[]: Did Seele have agents inside NERV?             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Feb 14, 2016 5:57 am I guess we could assume as much, but we don't exactly see anyone working for them other than Kaji, presembly those guards who brought Ritsuko to see them, and a few others if I remember correctly. However, wouldn't they have used them during the assault on NERV in EOE? I mean, rather than sneaking up behind some dude (however that happened) from under the shutter and entering the base, wouldn't they had had a guy on the inside open up all the access points for the JSSDF? That makes sense, right? How come we never saw anything like that?

[]: Re: Did Seele have agents inside NERV?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Feb 18, 2016 8:53 am Near the end of the series, Seele describe Nerv as having come far too much under the control of "one man" (Gendo). Gendo might have simply been too good at Seele's own game and ferreted out all the insiders who could have done what you describe. If we take Kaji as an example, he might have ultimately turned all their would-be moles against them similarly. Come to think of it, one could reasonably describe Kaji's death as a case of Gendo somehow being so persuasive as to convince Kaji to give his life to betray Seele.

[]: Did Seele have agents inside NERV?             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Feb 14, 2016 5:57 am I guess we could assume as much, but we don't exactly see anyone working for them other than Kaji, presembly those guards who brought Ritsuko to see them, and a few others if I remember correctly. However, wouldn't they have used them during the assault on NERV in EOE? I mean, rather than sneaking up behind some dude (however that happened) from under the shutter and entering the base, wouldn't they had had a guy on the inside open up all the access points for the JSSDF? That makes sense, right? How come we never saw anything like that?

Gendo might have simply been too good at Seele's own game and ferreted out all the insiders who could have done what you describe. If we take Kaji as an example, he might have ultimately turned all their would-be moles against them similarly. Come to think of it, one could reasonably describe Kaji's death as a case of Gendo somehow being so persuasive as to convince Kaji to give his life to betray Seele.
